message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata in issues.jsonl

Refresh dependency edges and status fields for tracked beads issues,
including the RaptorQ replication epic and related sub-beads, to
reflect current closure state and import-generated dependency links.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -619,7 +619,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2kc4","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ fountain-coded replication (UDP, changeset encoding, deterministic seed)","description":"Implement spec §3.4.2 fountain-coded replication protocol.

Spec essentials:
- Serialize changeset -> changeset_bytes.
- changeset_id := Trunc128(BLAKE3("fsqlite:replication:changeset:v1" || changeset_bytes)).
- seed := xxh3_64(changeset_id_bytes).
- Choose transport symbol size T_replication (MTU-driven).
- K_source = ceil(F/T_replication).
- V1 forbids multi-block (SBN>0): if K_source > 56,403, shard into multiple independent objects.
- Sender streams systematic symbols first (ESI 0..K-1), then repair symbols.

Epic closes when sender/receiver + packet format + apply semantics + tests exist.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:37:26.003138267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:42:25.574427974Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:42:25.574408918Z","close_reason":"All replication sub-beads are closed (sender, receiver, packet format, changeset model, simnetwork, perf path). Validated via fsqlite-core replication test lane and bd_26qk compliance tests; gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-15cu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jup","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lte","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1squ","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-26qk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","depends_on_id":"bd-xgoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2187,"issue_id":"bd-2kc4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Fountain-Coded Replication (Spec §3.4.2)

### Role In The System
This epic implements the *low-level* “send symbols until decode succeeds” protocol. It is intentionally narrower than the overall replication architecture (`bd-1hi.19`).

### Why Fountain Coding Matters
- Over lossy networks we avoid coordination loops (“send me ESI 17”) by allowing: “send any symbols for object X”.
- Stop-early makes bandwidth usage near-optimal: receiver stops sender once decode completes.

### Determinism / Explainability
- changeset identity and seed are deterministic:
  - `changeset_id := Trunc128(BLAKE3(domain_sep || bytes))`
  - `seed := xxh3_64(changeset_id_bytes)`
- Determinism lets us reproduce and debug replication failures from captured packets.

### Security (Optional)
Authenticated symbols: invalid tags are treated as erasures, not as fatal corruption.

### Done Means
- Packet format is versioned.
- Sender/receiver work under loss/reorder/dup.
- We can benchmark and justify defaults for `T` and overhead%.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:59:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kg2","title":"VDBE Insert must enforce default conflict as constraint error (no implicit upsert)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:15:06.631975745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T20:22:52.249927895Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T20:22:52.249908648Z","close_reason":"Completed: Insert default conflict path verified as constraint error and strengthened tests to ensure existing row is preserved in both btree and memdb execution paths.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2kkn4","title":"Witness Plane — SSI Conflict Detection","description":"Hot-plane indexing for O(log n) conflict detection in Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI) validation. SSI is the key correctness mechanism that makes page-level MVCC safe for concurrent writers — without it, write skew anomalies break serializable isolation.

## Background
Snapshot Isolation (SI) allows each transaction to see a consistent snapshot. However, SI allows write-skew anomalies where two concurrent transactions read overlapping data and write disjoint data, producing a result that no serial execution could produce. SSI detects these anomalies by tracking read-write antidependencies (rw-conflicts) and aborting transactions that form dangerous structures.

## Mathematical Foundation
Cahill, Rohm, Fekete 2009 ('Serializable Isolation for Snapshot Databases') proved that write-skew requires a cycle of rw-antidependencies in the serialization graph. Specifically, a 'dangerous structure' occurs when:
- T1 reads data that T2 writes (rw-antidependency T1 -> T2)
- T2 reads data that T1 writes (rw-antidependency T2 -> T1)
- This forms a cycle that could represent a non-serializable execution

The SSI validator tracks these antidependencies and aborts one transaction when a dangerous structure is detected. The 'first-committer-wins' (FCW) rule ensures that the first transaction to commit in a conflict pair succeeds.

## Key Paper
Cahill, Rohm, Fekete 2009: 'Serializable Isolation for Snapshot Databases'
Ports, Grittner 2012: 'Serializable Snapshot Isolation in PostgreSQL'

## Implementation Plan
1. Read-set tracking: record (txn_id, page_id) for every page read by each transaction
2. Write-set tracking: record (txn_id, page_id) for every page written by each transaction
3. Conflict detection: at commit time, check if the committing transaction's write-set intersects any concurrent transaction's read-set (and vice versa)
4. Hot-plane indexing: maintain an interval tree or B-tree index on (page_id, txn_timestamp) for O(log n) conflict lookups
5. Dangerous structure detection: when an rw-antidependency T1 -> T2 is detected, check if T2 -> T1 also exists (cycle = dangerous)
6. FCW resolution: abort the later-committing transaction in the cycle

## Connection to Other Features
- EBR must not free version chain entries that SSI still references for conflict detection
- Parallel WAL epoch boundaries interact with SSI commit ordering
- Evidence ledger records SSI abort decisions for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read-set and write-set tracked per transaction
- [ ] rw-antidependency cycle detection at commit time
- [ ] First-committer-wins conflict resolution
- [ ] O(log n) conflict detection via hot-plane indexing
- [ ] Write-skew anomalies correctly prevented
- [ ] False positive rate bounded (unnecessary aborts < 5%)
- [ ] Stress test: 100 concurrent writers with adversarial schedules","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:03.980631310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:02:51.781608359Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","correctness","differentiator","mvcc","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2kkn4.1","title":"Implement read-set/write-set tracking per transaction","description":"Implement per-transaction read-set and write-set tracking for SSI conflict detection. Every page access must be recorded for the SSI validator to detect rw-antidependencies at commit time.

Read-set tracking:
1. On every page read, record (page_id, version_ts) in the transaction's read-set
2. Read-set stored in a thread-local hash set (page_id -> version_ts)
3. Must handle: point reads (B-tree traversal), range scans (all pages in range), index lookups
4. Predicate locking optimization: for range scans, track the predicate (key range) instead of individual pages to detect phantom reads

Write-set tracking:
1. On every page write, record (page_id, old_version_ts, new_version_ts) in the write-set
2. Write-set stored in a thread-local hash map
3. Write-set is also needed for transaction rollback (undo the writes)

Space management:
- Read-sets can be large for analytical queries (scanning millions of pages)
- Optimization: bloom filter approximation for read-sets (false positives cause unnecessary aborts but no correctness issues)
- Configuration: EXACT_READ_SET (hash set, precise) vs BLOOM_READ_SET (bloom filter, compact but approximate)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every page read recorded in transaction read-set
- [ ] Every page write recorded in transaction write-set
- [ ] Predicate locking for range scans
- [ ] Bloom filter option for large read-sets
- [ ] Thread-local storage (no contention)
- [ ] Read-set/write-set correctly cleared on commit/abort
- [ ] Overhead: < 5% of transaction execution time for typical OLTP workloads
- [ ] Unit test: verify read-set/write-set contents for known access patterns","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:18.023619015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:49:31.459327963Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","mvcc","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2188,"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice landed for bd-2kkn4.1 (read/write set tracking):

Implemented:
- Added per-transaction page-access ledgers in :
  - 
  - 
  -  + helper methods for record/read/commit/clear
- Wired lifecycle instrumentation:
  -  now records visible read version + page witness key
  -  and  now record base version + page witness key
  -  now stamps  for tracked writes at commit publication
  -  now prunes write-version entries for pages rolled back
  -  now clears read/write tracking ledgers on finalization

New tests:
-

- Updated  to assert tracking clear + witness preservation

Validation evidence:

Remaining for full bead closure:
- Predicate-locking/range-scan integration
- Bloom read-set mode implementation and policy wiring
- End-to-end overhead characterization against <5% target","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:55:46Z"},{"id":2189,"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice landed for bd-2kkn4.1 (read/write set tracking):

Implemented:
- Added per-transaction page-access ledgers in `Transaction`:
  - `read_set_versions: page -> visible commit_seq`
  - `write_set_versions: page -> {old_version,new_version}`
  - `ReadSetStorageMode` + helper methods for record/read/commit/clear
- Wired lifecycle instrumentation:
  - `read_page` now records visible read version + page witness key
  - `write_page_serialized` and `write_page_concurrent` now record base version + page witness key
  - `publish_write_set` now stamps `new_version` for tracked writes at commit publication
  - `rollback_to_savepoint` now prunes write-version entries for pages rolled back
  - `release_all_resources` now clears read/write tracking ledgers on finalization

New tests:
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_page_access_tracking_records_versions`
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_clear_page_access_tracking`
- `lifecycle::tests::test_read_tracks_visible_version_and_witness_key`
- `lifecycle::tests::test_write_tracks_base_version_and_clears_tracking_on_commit`
- `lifecycle::tests::test_rollback_to_savepoint_prunes_write_version_tracking`
- Updated `lifecycle::tests::test_abort_concurrent_witnesses_preserved` to assert tracking clear + witness preservation

Validation evidence:
- `cargo fmt -p fsqlite-mvcc -- --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo check -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- Targeted tests passed:
  - `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc tracking -- --nocapture` (10 passed)
  - `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc test_read_tracks_visible_version_and_witness_key -- --nocapture` (1 passed)
  - `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc test_abort_concurrent_witnesses_preserved -- --nocapture` (1 passed)

Remaining for full bead closure:
- Predicate-locking/range-scan integration
- Bloom read-set mode implementation and policy wiring
- End-to-end overhead characterization against <5% target
","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:55:57Z"},{"id":2190,"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up slice for bd-2kkn4.1 landed (Bloom option + range-scan helper):

Implemented additionally:
- Added `ReadSetBloom` (simple Bloom filter) and wired it into `Transaction` as optional `read_set_bloom`
- `set_read_set_storage_mode(ReadSetStorageMode::Bloom)` now enables Bloom-backed membership tracking
- `record_page_read` now feeds the Bloom filter when enabled
- Added `read_set_maybe_contains(page)` to query exact/bloom membership safely
- Added `record_range_scan(&[PageNumber], version)` helper that registers range-scan witness pages and read-version entries
- `clear_page_access_tracking` now also resets Bloom state

New tests:
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_bloom_read_set_mode`
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_record_range_scan_adds_page_witnesses`

Validation evidence (post-follow-up):
- `cargo fmt -p fsqlite-mvcc -- --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo check -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc bloom_read_set_mode -- --nocapture` ✅
- `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc record_range_scan_adds_page_witnesses -- --nocapture` ✅
- `... cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc tracking -- --nocapture` ✅ (10 passed)

Remaining for full closure:
- integrate predicate/range tracking callsites from B-tree scan paths (currently helper exists, not yet wired in scan pipeline)
- provide thread-local/no-contention evidence for execution path
- overhead measurement (<5%) evidence for representative OLTP workload
","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:58:57Z"},{"id":2191,"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"`bv --robot-triage` refresh (data_hash=64c33df940212575) keeps `bd-2kkn4` in top-impact blockers list; continuing `bd-2kkn4.1` as the active lane I own.

New slice completed:
- Added thread-local read/write set mirrors keyed by `TxnToken` in `core_types.rs`:
  - `THREAD_LOCAL_READ_SET_VERSIONS`
  - `THREAD_LOCAL_WRITE_SET_VERSIONS`
- `Transaction::record_page_read` / `record_page_write` / `mark_page_write_committed` now synchronize both in-transaction maps and thread-local mirrors.
- Added thread-local introspection helpers:
  - `thread_local_read_version_for_page`
  - `thread_local_write_version_for_page`
  - `thread_local_read_set_len`
  - `thread_local_write_set_len`
- `clear_page_access_tracking` now clears thread-local mirrors for the transaction token.
- Added explicit range-scan lifecycle hook:
  - `TransactionManager::record_range_scan(&mut txn, &[PageNumber])`
  - records page witnesses + visible version per leaf page.

Tests added:
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_thread_local_mirrors_track_and_clear`
- `core_types::tests::test_transaction_thread_local_mirrors_are_thread_isolated`
- `lifecycle::tests::test_record_range_scan_tracks_all_pages`

Validation evidence:
- `cargo fmt -p fsqlite-mvcc -- --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc thread_local_mirrors -- --nocapture` ✅ (2 passed)
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc record_range_scan_tracks_all_pages -- --nocapture` ✅ (1 passed)
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-2kkn4 cargo check -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets` ✅

Remaining for bead closure:
- predicate/key-range witness integration from actual B-tree range-scan callsites (not only lifecycle hook)
- overhead evidence (<5%) for representative workload

 {"id":"bd-2kkn4.2","title":"Implement rw-antidependency cycle detection","description":"Implement the rw-antidependency cycle detection algorithm for SSI validation. This is the core of SSI — detecting 'dangerous structures' that indicate potential serialization anomalies.

Algorithm (per Cahill-Rohm-Fekete 2009):
At commit time for transaction T_commit:
1. For each page P in T_commit's write-set:
   a. Find all concurrent transactions T_reader that read P (rw-antidependency: T_reader -> T_commit)
   b. These are T_commit's 'inConflict' set
2. For each page P in T_commit's read-set:
   a. Find all concurrent transactions T_writer that wrote P (rw-antidependency: T_commit -> T_writer)
   b. These are T_commit's 'outConflict' set
3. Dangerous structure: if any T in inConflict ALSO has an outConflict involving T_commit (directly or transitively through another concurrent transaction)
4. If dangerous structure detected: abort T_commit (or the other transaction, per FCW rule)

Hot-plane indexing for O(log n) lookups:
- Maintain an index: page_id -> list of (txn_id, access_type, timestamp)
- Concurrent transactions: those with overlapping [begin_ts, commit_ts] intervals
- Use interval tree on transaction lifetimes for efficient overlap queries

Optimization:
- Only track conflicts for concurrent transactions (non-overlapping transactions cannot conflict)
- Prune conflict records eagerly when transactions commit (they can no longer form new conflicts)
- Per Ports-Grittner 2012: use a simplified two-hop check instead of full cycle detection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] rw-antidependency correctly identified (T reads page that T' writes)
- [ ] Dangerous structure detected (bidirectional rw-antidependency)
- [ ] O(log n) conflict lookup via hot-plane index
- [ ] Concurrent transaction identification via interval overlap
- [ ] Conflict record pruning on transaction completion
- [ ] Known write-skew scenarios correctly detected (bank transfer, doctor on-call)
- [ ] Unit tests with hand-crafted conflict schedules","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:31.878997188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:32:32.791420689Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","alien-concept","mvcc","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kkn4.3","title":"Implement first-committer-wins conflict resolution","description":"Implement the First-Committer-Wins (FCW) conflict resolution strategy for SSI. When a dangerous structure is detected, exactly one transaction must be aborted to prevent the serialization anomaly. FCW aborts the later-committing transaction.

FCW protocol:
1. When T_commit detects a dangerous structure involving T_other:
   a. If T_other has already committed: T_commit must abort (T_other wins)
   b. If T_other has not committed: T_commit can commit; T_other will be aborted when it tries to commit
   c. If both try to commit simultaneously: use a deterministic tiebreaker (lower txn_id wins)
2. Abort handling: 
   a. Roll back all writes (undo log)
   b. Release all locks
   c. Clear read-set and write-set
   d. Return SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT to caller
   e. Log abort reason to evidence ledger (galaxy-brain card with conflict details)
3. Retry policy: application retries the aborted transaction (new snapshot, new read-set)

Optimization — safe snapshots:
- PostgreSQL optimization: track 'safe' snapshots where no dangerous structure is possible
- If T_commit's snapshot is 'safe' (no concurrent transaction had both inConflict and outConflict), skip full validation
- This dramatically reduces validation overhead for non-conflicting transactions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FCW correctly aborts later-committing transaction in conflicts
- [ ] Deterministic tiebreaker for simultaneous commits
- [ ] Abort correctly rolls back all writes
- [ ] SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT returned to caller
- [ ] Evidence ledger records abort reason with conflict details
- [ ] Safe snapshot optimization reduces unnecessary validation
- [ ] Test: write-skew scenario, verify one transaction aborts
- [ ] Test: non-conflicting concurrent transactions both succeed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:44.172198439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:32:33.107673663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","mvcc","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kkn4.4","title":"Stress test: SSI correctness under adversarial schedules","description":"Comprehensive stress test suite for SSI correctness under adversarial transaction schedules. SSI must never allow a serialization anomaly to be committed — this is a correctness invariant, not a performance goal.

Test categories:

1. Classic write-skew (bank transfer):
   - Two accounts with constraint: balance_a + balance_b >= 0
   - T1 reads both, writes account_a (balance_a -= 100)
   - T2 reads both, writes account_b (balance_b -= 100)
   - Result: at most one should commit (the other violates constraint)

2. Doctor on-call:
   - Constraint: at least one doctor on-call
   - T1 reads all doctors, removes self from on-call (seeing others are on-call)
   - T2 reads all doctors, removes self from on-call (seeing others are on-call)
   - Result: at most one should commit

3. Batch insert + scan:
   - T1 scans all rows, computes aggregate
   - T2 inserts a new row that would change the aggregate
   - Phantom detection required

4. 100 concurrent writers:
   - Random read/write patterns on shared pages
   - Invariant: committed results must be equivalent to some serial execution
   - Use a serialization checker (e.g., check that committed transactions form a serializable history)

5. Adversarial scheduler:
   - Deliberately create rw-antidependency cycles
   - Verify exactly one transaction per cycle is aborted
   - Verify no false negatives (no anomaly escapes detection)

6. Long-running reader + writers:
   - One reader holds a snapshot for 10 seconds while writers continuously update
   - Reader must see a consistent snapshot; SSI must not abort the reader unnecessarily

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 test categories pass
- [ ] Zero serialization anomalies in committed transactions
- [ ] False positive abort rate < 5% (unnecessary aborts)
- [ ] 100-concurrent-writer stress test runs for 60 seconds without deadlock or livelock
- [ ] Serialization checker validates committed history
- [ ] CI integration (stress test at reduced scale)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:58.974004290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:32:34.029829522Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","mvcc","ssi","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kkn4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
